steps.title: Count each station type
steps.content: There are $7$ stations total. If $x$ stations are set up for Experiment A, then the remaining stations are set up for Experiment B. The number of Experiment B stations is therefore $7-x$.

steps.title: Write the total salt
steps.content:
Experiment A uses $9$ teaspoons of salt at each of the $x$ stations, so that part requires $9x$ teaspoons. Experiment B uses $5$ teaspoons at each of the $7-x$ stations, so the total expression is:
$$9x+5(7-x)$$

steps.title: Simplify the expression
steps.content: Distribute the $5$ across $(7-x)$, then combine the $x$-terms. This writes the total number of teaspoons in simplified form: $$9x+5(7-x)=9x+35-5x=4x+35$$ The answer is C.
